---
title: "Install Fails: md5 problem with image... but cd passes md5 validation!"
date: 2006-02-25
forum: Installation &amp; Upgrades
---

### Post by rjharrison on 2006-02-25
Hi there.

I'm having a hard time installing Unbuntu, and i'm stuck.

I downloaded the ISO via torrent and successfully validated the MD5 checksum of the downloaded .iso file.

I then burned the iso to a cd using Nero on x8 speed. I used IsoBuster to validate the checksum of my new cd - and it was valid.

# MD5 checksum generated by IsoBuster 1.9.0.3 ([http://www.isobuster.com](http://www.isobuster.com))
dbaabff6a39eb4ceb2ebc4706304d670 *CD.cue
126751a2dc5528c2f9044d9e4ee36d61 *CD.tao
126751A2DC5528C2F9044D9E4EE36D61 *ubuntu-5.10-install-i386.iso
126751a2dc5528c2f9044d9e4ee36d61  ubuntu-5.10-install-i386.iso (from download site)

I start the installation process, selecting the various options suitable for me, and get to the "Installing Base System" part. Everything is going fine until it comes to the kernel where it spits an error about not being able to install. I tried a couple of times, same error comes up.

So i tried verifiying the CD's integrity... and it fails! (having coming up valid md5 on both download and burn). I check the logs and find the following:-

```
The following NEW packages will be installed:
  linux-386 linux-image-2.6.12-9-386 linux-image-386
  linux-restricted-modules-2.6.12-9-386 linux-restricted-modules-386
  linux-restricted-modules-common nvidia-kernel-common
Failed to fetch file:///cdrom/pool/main/l/linux-source-2.6.12/linux-image-2.6.12-9-386_2.6.12-9.23_i386.deb  MD5Sum mismatch
Failed to fetch file:///cdrom/pool/restricted/l/linux-restricted-modules-2.6.12/linux-restricted-modules-2.6.12-9-386_2.6.12.4-11_i386.deb  MD5Sum mismatch

```

So my cd would appear to validate against the MD5, as would my downloaded iso, yet it does not pass the internal integrity check and the above md5sum mismatch error is present.

Argh! What's going on!? :)

---

### Post by Greg2 on 2006-02-25
[QUOTE=rjharrison]
I downloaded the ISO via torrent and successfully validated the MD5 checksum of the downloaded .iso file.

I then burned the iso to a cd using Nero on x8 speed. I used IsoBuster to validate the checksum of my new cd - and it was valid.[/QUOTE]
I’m not sure why the md5sum didn’t catch the error... but I’m not familiar with IsoBuster? I would however suggest reducing the burning rate to x4 or less to prevent any errors on your CD.

### Post by rjharrison on 2006-02-25
[http://www.mepislovers-wiki.org/index.php?title=Checking_the_integrity_of_a_Live_CD](http://www.mepislovers-wiki.org/index.php?title=Checking_the_integrity_of_a_Live_CD)

That's the technique i used to verify the burn.

I've made many new coasters whilst trying to install (tried burning another distro iso and had failed burns, went down to 1x speed... no joy), so i'm guessing my CDRW drive on my XP box is fubared. I've ordered a printed Ubuntu disc from [http://www.linuxmedia.co.uk/](http://www.linuxmedia.co.uk/) as a last resort.

Still, strange that the CD appeared to validate... i'm wondering if it might be a problem with the cd-rom on the install machine.

---

### Post by Greg2 on 2006-02-25
[QUOTE=rjharrison]
I've made many new coasters whilst trying to install (tried burning another distro iso and had failed burns, went down to 1x speed... no joy), so i'm guessing my CDRW drive on my XP box is fubared.[/QUOTE]
Some thoughts...

Do you have the latest firmware for your CDRW?
Did you try a different CD media?
Did you ever successfully burn an iso with your Nero on that drive?

### Post by rjharrison on 2006-02-27
CDs from linuxmedia arrived this morning and i get the same problem; when it tries to access the "linux-image-2.6.12-9-386_2.6.12-9.23_i386.deb" file my CD/DVD rom drive starts to whirr like crazy before the error pops up.

I am definitely using valid install media now, i'm guessing the problem is with the drive on the install machine - perhaps it's fubared and cannot read certain sectors properly.

Time to replace it...

---

### Post by rjharrison on 2006-02-27
... grrr, dvd drive was indeed 'faulty' (or certainly the cause of the problem). I plugged in an old drive i had lying around and it passed the integrity check first time.

The drive in question was a Plextor PX-130A/T3PBP ([http://www.dabs.com/productview.aspx?quicklinx=3P4S)](http://www.dabs.com/productview.aspx?quicklinx=3P4S)).

---
